entry.title: SAP ERP公有云预算和计划功能 - 手工导入计划收入数据
entry.content:
背景

很多公有云的实施商和客户有SAP ECC 的实施和使用经验。SAP ECC有一些计划的功能比如：SOP(Sales and operation planning)的功能，基于成本获利能力分析的收入计划功能，成本中心费用计划和通过活动类型计划的消耗数量进一步计算活动类型的计划价格等等。这些功能在S/4HANA 私有云中依然可用（默认处于非激活的状态），但在公有云中这些功能已经不存在了，相关的功能需要在SAP Analytics Cloud和Business Integration Planning这两个SAP产品中实现，或者在非SAP的计划工具软件中实现，然后把计划数据传入S/4HANA Cloud中。

有三种方式可以在S/4HANA系统中生成计划数据

  1. 使用 Fiori 应用导入财务计划数据通过Excel模板导入
  2. 使用Financial Plan Data-Write API
  3. 集成SAC 计划

系统操作

本系列博客将陆续介绍SAP产品的计划功能和S/4HANA公有云的集成。本文将介绍第一种方式通过Excel模板导入计划数据到S/4HANA系统中

打开Fiori 应用导入财务计划数据

可以看到有多个模板

  • 成本中心计划模板用来导入成本中心费用数据，计划的数据后续可以通过Fiori应用管理分配在成本中心中进行分配。
  • 成本中心预算编制模板用来导入成本中心预算数据，后续使用Fiori应用成本中心预算报表查看成本中心的实际和预算的数据
  • 市场细分计划模板用来导入计划的收入。在下载模板的基础之上，可以添加更多的标准字段和扩展字段。参考Help Portal文档：用于财务计划数据导入的字段(此功能类似于在ECC时代基于成本获利能力分析输入或导入计划收入的功能)

后面我们介绍一下市场细分计划模板导入计划收入的流程。

假设我们在SAP系统外已经做好了未来的销售计划，计划数据已经转换成导入模板的格式，在默认导入模板中填加了利润中心，获利能力产品，以公司代码货币计的金额，公司代码货币字段

Fiori 应用导入财务计划数据 点击浏览CSV文件，选择已经准备好的上传文件。可以先测试导入源文件，确定没有报错并且显示将要导入的数据和Excel中准备的数据相符。点击导入源文件。计划数据成功导入后可以点击显示财务计划数据。

也可以使用Fiori 应用市场细分-计划和实际查看收入的计划和实际的数据。

计划和实际的数据通过CDS View I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube和I_ActualPlanLineItemSemTag查看，可以用来进一步制作嵌入式分析的报表。

小结

本文概览的介绍了计划数据在S/4HANA公有云系统中生成的方式，详细介绍了Excel导入计划收入数据的过程和报表的查看方法，后续博客还会介绍SAP产品其他的计划功能。欢迎关注和讨论，并查看其他的论坛博客。

entry.title: Europe´s 1st Two-Tier SAP Cloud ERP Summit
entry.content:
WALLDORF - On February 18th and 19th, we held the 1st European Two-Tier Cloud ERP Summit, a 1,5-day workshop for partners focusing on connected Large Enterprise scenarios updating also on the future GTM with SAP Business Suite. 

 The purpose of the event was to educate and raise the attention to watch out for such scenarios in architecture discussions with larger clients. It also provided great showcases and increased the credibility and trust in Two-tier ERP operating models. 

 With over 60 participants from EMEA & MEE countries mainly from GSSP (Deloitte, IBM, Accenture etc.) as well as some architects from European VAR´s we had a great turn-up ratio of 90%. 

 The workshop provided our partners with insights and knowledge around the SAP Two-Tier ERP Cloud strategy and popular scenarios, whilst we also deep dived into the architectural guidelines and integrations. Especially, the customer proof points from Bosch, LanXess and Accelleron presented by our partners Deloitte and Accenture resonated very well with our partners. 

 The workshop also helped us foster open and trusted conversations during in-person exchanges between SAP subject matter experts and partner leadership teams.  

Due to high demand among our GSSP´s, we will keep the momentum and plan to provide this workshop in more regions. Let us know if you want to contribute to the next Summits and please contact Nitin Vohra or Nilguen Atasoy.

 “The SAP Two tier Cloud summit allowed a common facilitated workspace where vendors could come together, discuss and learn about how to make SAP S/4 HANA public cloud a success among their clients by learning from each other and SAP. It was valuable in both generating confidence & providing new insights.” - Jonathan Eaton, Senior Specialist Lead | Deloitte Certified Chief Architect |

Deloitte

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: View Business Event Logs
entry.content:
This analytical application lets you review business events logged in Business Event Logging.

Screen Areas

Information filter

You can use the information filter at the top of the screen to select which data is displayed in the chart and table. This allows you to view your business event log data in various ways. You must enter the Object and Time Interval, as they are mandatory filters. Additionally, you can use other filters such as “Object Component,” “User,” and so on. The Producer Namespace helps you differentiate between SAP-provided and customer events. SAP-provided business events always carry the producer namespace “sap.s4.beh.”

Fig. 2: Filter bar

Visual filter

Visual filter provides a graphical interface for interacting with and filtering your data. It allows you to view and filter the frequency of events based on "Year and Calendar Week," as well as by "Object Component," such as Item or Schedule Line.

Fig. 3: Visual filter bar

Bar chart

The bar chart offers a concise overview of the frequency of specific events. By selecting a bar, you can filter the entries in the table. Additionally, utilize the View By filter to explore different dimensional data. You also have the option to change the chart type from the default bar chart to other types of charts.

Fig. 4: Bar chart in Hybrid view

Fig. 5: Bar chart in Chart-only view

The "Hybrid view," also referred to as "Chart and Table View," is the default setting that displays both the chart and the table simultaneously. Additionally, you have the option to toggle between "Chart-only view" and "Table-only view" within the chart area.

Fig. 6: Toolbar in chart area

Table

The table located at the bottom of the screen provides additional details regarding the logged business events, including Business Event Type, Object IDs, Performed By, and Producer Namespace. In the "Settings" button, you have the option to customize the displayed columns to include information such as Object Keys, Node ID, Source of Data, and more. Additionally, you can utilize the "Group By" and "Sort by" features for the columns.

Upon selecting a table entry, you will be directed to the details page of the business event. Selecting the Object IDs gives you an overview of all the events for an object (components), and the Performed By column provides access to the user contact card.

Fig. 7: Table in Table-only view

Details page

The specifics of the event are presented on the object page. This page provides a summary of the key properties associated with the event.

  • Event type and version
  • Object component and IDs
  • Date of execution
  • User
  • Version and release status of the event (mostly “released” and “deprecated”
  • Source system
  • Transaction codes, if available
  • Source of Data: currently only “Business Event” = externally released business events by SAP, or customer events with event binding

A raised event may include Event Qualifiers and Field Changes, which are presented in separate tables.

Event qualifiers are occasionally used in generic business events. Their values can offer more precise semantics to the business event.

Field Changes provides a comprehensive list of all value modifications recorded in the logged business events. For events with a deep structure, this table additionally displays the path to the changed field.

EventUUID serves as a distinct identifier for the event, while NodeID identifies a specific instance of an object component, such as a Sales Order Item. Additionally, the Transaction ID denotes the technical session(SAP LUW) during which the event was executed within the system.

Fig. 8: Details page

To enable logging of all business event data in the BEL logging configuration, check the All Data option for the SAP Object Type. You can access the logged business event data by clicking the "View Business Event Data" button on the details page.

Fig. 9: Business event data in a popup

Release Information

 

ProductAvailability
SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition2411 and later
SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition2021 FPS02* and later
SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise)2021 FPS02* and later
SAP BTP ABAP Environment2411

* In SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, the functionality of logging and viewing all business data is only available in 2023.

Fiori Apps Library Reference

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S34

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S29PCE

SAP S/4 HANA (On-Premise): https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6021')/S29OP

entry.title: SAP ERP公有云台湾本地化-代扣税实施建议
entry.content:
引言：由于SAP ERP公有云标准并没有提供台湾本地化的代扣税功能，本篇博客主要是基于对其他国家的参考来实现的，欢迎各位读者能够提出更多的意见和建议。

目录：

  • 代扣税介绍
  • 过账代扣税的业务场景
  • 系统中配置代扣税
  • 代扣税功能演示
  • 代扣税报表
  • 结论
  • 参考信息

___________________________________________________________________________

  • 代扣税介绍

代扣税扣减由适用国家/地区的法定要求决定，因此纯粹是一个本地化主题。在SAP ERP公有云中，通过范围项目 1J2-高级合规性报表进行处理。

代扣税是在某些国家/地区开始付款流时收取的税。通常，应纳税方不会直接向税务部门缴纳代扣税。当有权扣减代扣税的客户支付应缴纳代扣税的供应商发票时，客户会从付款金额中减去代扣税金额。然后，客户直接向相应的税务部门缴纳代扣税。但对于自代扣例外，在这种情况下，受税款约束的供应商有权直接向相关部门支付税款。

在台湾地区，代扣税是公司代扣的居民或非居民收入付款（付款方）的金额。如果您要支付非本地（国外）供应商，则需要预扣发票金额的一定百分比并向税务局支付税表，以及要支付给国外供应商的余额。

涵盖如下付款：

  • 股息
  • 利息
  • 租赁收入
  • 版税
  • 技术费用、服务付款
  • 工资和薪水
  • 佣金

台湾的代扣税制度适用于居民和非居民所赚取的各种收入，按收入类型和纳税人的居住身份确定税率和规定。每种类型的付款都适用不同的代扣税率，如果台湾与非居民（外国方）是税收居民的国家/地区之间存在双重税收协议，则优惠税率可能适用。下表详细说明了台湾代扣税系统中不同付款的相关表单。

收入类型

居民个人

非居民个人

外国公司

股息

20%

21%

21%

利息

10%

20%

20%

租赁收入

10%

20%

20%

版税

10%

20%

20%

专业服务费

10%

20%(有例外)

20%

薪水和工资

6%-18%

18% (如果<183 天停留)

不适用

佣金

-

20%

20%

参考文末链接【3】

代扣税举例：台湾公司向外国公司采购专业服务费税前金额为 5000 美元，外国公司将承担预扣税，因此，台湾公司只需要向国外公司支付4000美 元，扣除的1000美 元（5000 美元的 20%）应该支付给税务部门。

  • 过账代扣税的业务场景
  1. 根据每个国家/地区的法律要求，在不同阶段计算代扣税并将其过账到相应的代扣税科目。通常，代扣税是在过账付款的同时过账的。换言之，付款（应付账款）或收款（应收账款）将减去代扣税金额。
  2. 在应付账款中，供应商应纳税，公司有义务扣减代扣税并代表供应商将其支付给税务部门。在应收账款中，公司本身需要缴税。与此公司有业务往来的客户扣减代扣税，并代表公司将其支付给税务部门。在这两种情况下，应纳税人/实体的业务伙伴都会扣减税款并将其支付给税务部门。
  3. 在某些国家/地区（例如巴西、菲律宾和西班牙），代扣税可以或必须在过账发票时过账。这意味着应收账款或应付账款金额会减去代扣税金额。
  4. 在某些国家/地区（例如阿根廷和意大利），某些业务交易需要其他类型的过账。代扣税将作为抵销分录过账到费用科目，而不是按代扣税金额减少的付款金额。维护代扣税代码时为此进行相关设置。
  5. 重置已清算项目时，如果清算凭证包含代扣税数据，则会自动冲销。对于含累计的代扣税类型，如果冲销付款凭证，则会调整累计基础金额。
  6. 本博客中，会示例应付发票时点过账代扣税及付款时点过账代扣税这两种场景。

 

  • 系统中配置代扣税

     1.  激活代扣税

要在SAP ERP公有云中使用代扣税（也称为扩展代扣税），必须先将其激活。

2. 定义代扣税代码– 106576

代扣税代码用于标识不同的代扣税类型。

3. 为付款过账定义代扣税类型– 104226

预扣税可以在两个时间点扣除；可以在开票时或付款时扣除。因此，对于此代扣税类型，将创建一个代扣税类型用于付款，另一个用于发票。

4. 为发票过账定义代扣税类型– 104225

预扣税可以在两个时间点扣除；可以在开票时或付款时扣除。因此，对于此代扣税类型，将创建一个代扣税类型用于付款，另一个用于发票。

5. 为公司代码分配代扣税类型– 103914

6. 定义代扣税码- 104227

在代扣税代码中，维护不同的税率并维护对应扣减的基础。

7. 定义代扣税码生效最小/最大限定金额 – 103915

如果需要，可以定义代扣税码生效最小/最大限定金额，如果不指定任何金额，则代表代扣税没有上下限金额的限定。

8. 将代扣税代码分配给代扣税码 – 105553

9. 为代扣税类型分配汇率类型 - 104400

10. 为过账代扣税分配总账科目 – 100297

11. 检查代扣税配置

 

  • 代扣税功能演示

SAP ERP公有云中代扣税也称为扩展代扣税，包含代扣税类型和代扣税代码。代扣税类型表示基本计算规则。代扣税代码代表这些规则的特定功能，尤其是百分率。

如果特定交易需要多种代扣税，则在SAP ERP公有云系统中通过定义多个代扣税类型来涵盖这一点。输入行项目时，可以为每种代扣税类型输入代扣税数据。但请注意，如果发票中有两组或多组代扣税代码和类型，则必须决定所有建议的代扣税代码和类型是否与交易相关。如果不计算和过账某些建议的代扣税，则必须移除与交易无关的代扣税代码。

 

1. 在发票时点过账代扣税

1). 维护业务伙伴主数据

从如上截屏中能够看到供应商代扣税类型设定为发票过账时点生成代扣税行项目，代扣税码为T1即20%的代扣税率。

2). 过账供应商发票

查看模拟生成的财务分录：

查看到生成了代扣税凭证分录，以抵减应付账款金额。

2. 付款时点过账代扣税

1). 维护业务伙伴主数据

从如上截屏中能够看到供应商代扣税类型设定为付款时点生成代扣税行项目，代扣税码为T1即20%的代扣税率。

2). 过账供应商发票

查看模拟生成的财务分录：

3). 对供应商发票付款

模拟付款凭证，可以看到付款时点生成了下图的代扣税行项目。

目前，对于台湾的代扣税过账时点，客户可以根据需求参考应用对应的场景。

 

  • 代扣税报表

对于某些国家/地区，代扣税报表根据从供应商凭证和客户凭证中收集的信息生成，例如已清算发票、供应商贷记凭证、预付定金和已清算贷记凭证。系统生成可提交给政府部门的文本文件。某些国家/地区的凭证和报表合规性(DRC)功能被激活后，可以使用运行法定报表生成代扣税报表。

例如，如下是美国的代扣税报表：

Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report for the US

如下是比利时的代扣税报表信息：

Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

但是目前SAP ERP公有云还没有为台湾提供标准的代扣税申报报表，可以使用如下CDS View来获取相应的代扣税信息：

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax ：供应商代扣税
  • I_CustomerWithTax ：客户代扣税
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode ：代扣税码
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01 (供应商发票的代扣税)

现在已经有台湾客户发送代扣税申报报表的需求给SAP ERP公有云产品部门，期待未来台湾本地化功能中代扣税申报报表的呈现。客户请求详细请参考如下链接：

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

在CE2502版本开始SAP ERP公有云提供了一个新的应用程序“代扣税分析”，该应用程序能够回答一个问题，那就是“如何计算此交易的代扣税？”，如下是德国公司代码的范例：

对于该应用的信息，请参考帮助文档：Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis | SAP Help Portal

然而，该应用暂时不支持台湾地区使用，会提示如下信息：

期待未来该应用可以开放给台湾使用。

 

  • 结论

在台湾地区，代扣税适用于不同收入类型，例如台湾地区内部生成的股息、租金、利息和版税。其重要性在于确保及时征税、简化税务管理、执行遵约。

在每月的第十天之前，代扣代缴税申报方应向财政部缴纳上个月扣缴的税款，并于下一纳税年度的 1 月之前向税务局提交扣缴证明，具体说明上一年缴纳税款和扣缴的税款。如果在 1 月份有连续的公共节假日，则提交扣缴证明的时期应延长至 2 月 5 日。对于向非居民或在 ROC 中没有常设机构的外国企业支付的收入，代扣代缴税代理应在预扣税款后向财政部支付扣缴的税款，并在扣缴后十天内向税务局提交代扣代缴税报表和收据。参考文末链接【4】

与台湾许多税种一样，对税务申报的误解或错过截止期限，都会给在台内外经营的外国公司造成经济损失。

 

  • 参考信息
  1. Help Portal Documentation | Withholding Tax
  2.  Virtual Data Model and CDS Views | Withholding Tax for Supplier Invoice
  3. Taiwan withholding Tax Guide | Tax | SongjerCPA
  4. When shall a withholding agent file returns and make payments of tax withheld?
  5. Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report in the US
  6. Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

entry.title: SAP ERP公有雲台灣本土化 - 扣繳稅方案建議
entry.content:
簡介：由於SAP ERP公有雲標準並未提供針對台灣本土化的扣繳稅功能，因此此部落格主要根據來自其他國家的參考內容，歡迎讀者提出其他意見及建議。

目錄：

  • 扣繳稅簡介
  • 過帳扣繳稅的方案
  • 組態系統中的扣繳稅
  • 扣繳稅功能展示
  • 扣繳稅報表
  • 結論
  • 參考資訊

_______________________________________________________________

  • 扣繳稅簡介

由於扣繳稅扣除是根據適用國家的法定需求所規定，因此這純粹是本土化主題。在SAP ERP公有雲中，這會透過範圍項目 1J2- advanced Compliance Reporting 處理。

扣繳稅為部份國家/地區付款流程開始時收取的稅。一般而言，課稅方不會直接向稅捐機構支付扣繳稅。當授權扣除扣繳稅的客戶支付應付扣繳稅的供應商發票時，客戶會從付款金額中扣除扣繳稅額。接著，客戶會直接將扣繳稅支付給個別稅捐機構。然而，針對自行扣繳，視情況而定，供應商有權直接向機關支付稅款。

在台灣，扣繳稅為貴公司扣繳的居民或非居民所得稅 (付款人) 金額。若您正在支付非本地 (國外) 供應商，則必須扣繳發票金額的特定百分比並支付稅務表單給稅捐機關，以及要支付給國外供應商的餘額。

涵蓋下列付款：

  • 股利
  • 利息
  • 租賃收入
  • 版稅
  • 技術費用、服務付款
  • 工資和薪資
  • 佣金

台灣的代扣稅制，適用於居民與非居民所得的各種收入，按納稅人的收入類型和居住狀態確定稅率和規定。不同扣繳稅率適用於每種支付，如果台灣與非居民（外國人）為稅務居民的國家簽訂雙重徵稅協議，則適用優惠稅率。下列表格詳細說明台灣扣繳稅系統中不同付款的相關表單。

收入類型

居民個人

非居民個人

外國公司

股利

20%

21%

21%

利息

10%

20%

20%

租賃收入

10%

20%

20%

版稅

10%

20%

20%

專業費用

10%

20%〈有異常〉

20%

薪資和工資

6%-18%

18% (若 <183 天住宿)

不適用

佣金

-

20%

20%

參考連結 [3]

扣繳稅款範例：台灣企業向稅前5000元的外商購買專業服務，外商將承擔扣繳稅，因此台灣企業只需向外國企業支付4000美元，應向稅務機關繳納1000美元（折合5,000美元20%）的扣除。

  • 過帳扣繳稅的方案
  1. 根據各國家/地區的法律規定，扣繳稅會在不同階段進行計算，並過帳至對應的扣繳稅科目。作為規則，扣繳稅會在過帳付款的同時過帳。換句話說，扣繳稅額會減少付款 (應付帳款) 或收款 (應收帳款)。
  2. 在應付帳款中，供應商可稅，且公司有義務代表供應商扣除扣繳稅並支付給稅捐機構。在應收帳款中，公司本身需要課稅。與此公司進行業務的客戶可扣除扣繳稅，並代表公司支付給稅捐機構。在這兩種情況下，應稅人/實體的業務夥伴皆會扣除稅並支付給稅捐機構。
  3. 在某些國家/地區 (例如巴西、菲律賓和西班牙)，扣繳稅可在過帳發票時或必須過帳。這表示扣繳稅額會減少應收帳款或應付帳款金額。
  4. 在部份國家/地區 (例如阿根廷和義大利)，部份商業交易需要其他類型的過帳。扣繳稅會過帳為費用科目的沖銷分錄，而非扣繳稅金額減少的付款金額。維護扣繳稅碼時，您可進行相關設定。
  5. 重設結清的項目時，若結清文件包含扣繳稅資料，則會自動迴轉。針對含累計的扣繳稅類型，若已迴轉付款文件，則會調整累計基礎金額。
  6. 在此部落格中，範例 A/P 發票中會顯示付款時過帳扣繳稅和過帳扣繳稅的兩個方案。
  • 組態系統中的扣繳稅

1. 啟用扣繳稅 - 104228

若要在SAP ERP公有雲中使用扣繳稅 (也稱為延伸扣繳稅)，您必須先啟用。

2. 定義扣繳稅碼 – 106576

扣繳稅碼用於識別不同的扣繳稅類型。

3. 定義付款過帳的扣繳稅類型 – 104226

扣繳稅可於兩點扣除；可於開立發票時或付款時扣除。因此，針對此扣繳稅類型，系統會針對付款建立一個扣繳稅類型，而另一個用於發票。

4. 定義發票過帳的扣繳稅類型 – 104225

扣繳稅可於兩點扣除；可於開立發票時或付款時扣除。因此，針對此扣繳稅類型，系統會針對付款建立一個扣繳稅類型，而另一個用於發票。

5. 將扣繳稅類型指派給公司代碼 – 103914

6. 定義扣繳稅碼 - 104227

在扣繳稅碼中維護不同稅率，並維護對應扣除的基準。

7. 定義扣繳稅碼有效最小/最大限制金額 – 103915

您可視需要定義扣繳稅碼有效的最小/最大限制金額，或者若未指定金額，則扣繳稅額沒有下限和下限的金額限制。

8. 將扣繳稅碼指派給扣繳稅代碼 – 105553

9. 將匯率類型指派給扣繳稅類型 - 104400

10. 指派過帳扣繳稅的總帳科目 – 100297

11. 檢查扣繳稅組態

①檢查公司代碼

②檢查業務夥伴

③檢查扣繳稅類型

④模擬計算

 

  • 扣繳稅功能展示

SAP ERP公有雲中的扣繳稅也稱為延伸扣繳稅，並包含扣繳稅類型和扣繳稅碼。扣繳稅類型代表基礎計算規則。扣繳稅碼代表這些規則的特定功能，特別是百分比。

若特定交易需要一個以上的扣繳稅，則 SAP 系統中會透過定義數個扣繳稅類型來涵蓋此扣繳稅。輸入細目時，您可以輸入每個扣繳稅類型的扣繳稅資料。請注意，若發票中有兩個或多個扣繳稅代碼和類型集，則必須決定所有建議的扣繳稅碼和類型是否與交易相關。若未計算並過帳部份建議的扣繳稅，則必須移除與交易無關的扣繳稅碼。

1. 於發票點過帳扣繳稅方案

    1) 維護業務夥伴主檔資料

在上述擷取畫面中，您可看到供應商扣繳稅類型已設為發票過帳的時間點，以產生扣繳稅代碼 T1 (扣繳稅率為 20%) 的扣繳稅明細項目。

2). 過帳供應商發票

檢視模擬產生的財務輸入項：

您會看到扣繳稅文件輸入項已產生，以減少應付金額。

2. 於付款點過帳扣繳稅

    1). 維護業務夥伴主檔資料

在上述擷取畫面中，您可看到已將供應商扣繳稅類型設定為付款點，以產生扣繳稅代碼 T1 的扣繳稅明細項目 (扣繳稅率為 20%)。

2). 過帳供應商發票

檢視模擬產生的財務輸入項：

3). 供應商發票付款

模擬付款文件，您可在下列數字中查看付款點產生扣繳稅明細項目。

針對台灣的扣繳稅過帳點，客戶目前可根據需求參考對應方案。

 

  • 扣繳稅報表

針對部份國家/地區，扣繳稅報表會根據自供應商和客戶文件收集的資訊產生，例如結清發票、供應商貸項通知單、訂金和結清的貸項通知單。系統會產生可提交給政府機關的內文檔案。部份國家/地區的 Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) 功能啟用後，您可使用 [執行法定報表] 產生扣繳稅報表。

例如，美國的扣繳稅報表如下：

美國扣繳稅 (1099-MISC) 報表

比利時的扣繳稅申報資訊如下：

比利時扣繳稅報表

然而，SAP 尚未提供台灣的標準扣繳稅申報報表，且可使用下列 CDS 檢視以取得對應扣繳稅資訊：

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax：供應商扣繳稅
  • I_CustomerWithTax：客戶扣繳稅
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode：扣繳稅碼
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01: 供應商發票上的扣繳稅

現在台灣客戶需要將扣繳稅申報報表傳送給SAP ERP公有雲產品部門，並期待未來台灣本土化功能中的扣繳稅申報報表呈現。如需客戶請求，請參閱下列詳細連結：

改善請求明細 - 客戶影響

從 CE2502 開始，SAP ERP公有雲提供新的應用程式「扣繳稅分析」，可回答問題「要如何計算此交易的扣繳稅？」下列是德國公司代碼的範例：

如需有關應用程式的資訊，請參閱輔助說明文件： 扣繳稅計算分析 | SAP Help Portal

然而，此應用程式暫時不支援台灣使用，提示下列資訊：

希望未來將開放該應用程式在台灣使用。

 

  • 結論

在台灣地區，扣繳稅適用於不同的收入類型，例如台灣內部產生的股利、租金、利息和權利金。要確保及時納稅，簡化稅收管理，落實各項合規制度。

在每月第十天前，扣繳稅申報人應當向財政部繳納稅款上個月，並在下一納稅年度1月前向稅務機關提交扣繳證明，具體規定上一年度繳納稅款和代扣稅款。如果1月連續有國定假日，則提交扣繳憑證的期間應延長至2月5日。對於向非居民或者外國企業繳納的在中華民國境內沒有常設機構的收入，扣繳代理人應當在代扣稅款後向財政部繳納代扣稅款，並在代扣後十日內向稅務機關提交扣繳稅款申報和收據。參考連結 [4]

像台灣很多稅種一樣，對報稅的誤解和錯過期限，可能會給台灣境內外經營的外商造成經濟損失。

 

  • 參考資訊
  • Help Portal 紀錄文件 | 扣繳稅
  •  虛擬資料模型和 CDS 檢視 | 供應商發票扣繳稅
  • 台灣扣繳稅指南 | 稅 | SongjerCPA
  • 持有代理人何時應退回並支付扣繳稅？
  • 美國扣繳稅 (1099-MISC) 報表
  • 比利時扣繳稅報表

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Taiwan Localization - Workaround Solution for Withholding Tax
entry.content:
Introduction: Since the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition standard does not provide withholding tax functionality localized for Taiwan, this blog is based mainly on references from other countries, and readers are welcome to make additional comments and suggestions.

Catalog:

  • Withholding Tax Introduction
  • Scenario for Posting Withholding Tax
  • Configure Withholding Tax in the System
  • Withholding Tax Demos
  • Report for Withholding Tax
  • Conclusion
  • Reference Information

______________________________________________________________

 

  • Withholding Tax Introduction

Withholding Tax deduction is dictated by the statutory requirements of the applicable country and hence it is purely a localization topic. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition it is addressed through the scope item 1J2- Advance Compliance Reporting.

Withholding tax is the tax that is charged when the payment flow starts in some countries/regions. Generally, the taxable party does not pay withholding tax directly to the tax authority. When a customer authorized to deduct withholding tax pays a vendor invoice that is subject to withholding tax, the customer subtracts the withholding tax amount from the payment amount. The customer then pays the withholding tax directly to the tax authority. However, for self-withholding, suppliers subject to taxes have the right to pay taxes directly to the authorities in this case.

In Taiwan, withholding tax is the amount of resident or non-resident income payments (payers) withheld by your company. If you are paying a non-local (foreign) supplier, you need to withhold a certain percentage of the invoice amount and pay the tax form to the tax office, as well as the balance to be paid to the foreign supplier.

The following payments are covered:

  • Dividends
  • Interest
  • Rental Income
  • Royalties
  • Payments for technical fees, services
  • Wages and Salaries
  • Commissions

Taiwan's withholding tax system applies to the various income earned by residents and non-residents, determining tax rates and regulations by income type and residence status of taxpayers. Different withholding tax rates apply for each type of payment and if there is a double tax agreement between Taiwan and a country in which a non-resident (foreign party) is a tax resident, the reduced tax rate may apply. The following table details the relevant forms for the different payments in the Taiwan withholding tax system.

Reference link [3]

Withholding tax example: Taiwan companies purchase professional services from foreign companies with an amount of $5,000 before tax, foreign companies will bear withholding tax, so Taiwan companies only need to pay US$4,000 to foreign companies, and the deduction of 1,000 US dollars (20% of $5,000 USD) should be paid to the tax authorities.

  • Scenario for Posting Withholding Tax
  1. Depending on the legal requirements of each country/region, withholding tax is calculated at different stages and posted to the corresponding withholding tax accounts. As a rule, withholding tax is posted at the same time as the payment is posted. In other words, the payment (payable) or incoming payment (receivable) is reduced by the withholding tax amount.
  2. In accounts payable, the supplier is excisable and the company is obliged to deduct withholding tax and pay it to the tax authorities on behalf of the supplier. In accounts receivable, the company itself is subject to tax. Customers who do business with this company deduct withholding tax and pay them to the tax authorities on behalf of the company. In both cases, the business partner of the taxable person/entity deducts the tax and pays it to the tax authority.
  3. In some countries/regions, such as Brazil, the Philippines, and Spain, withholding tax can or must be posted when the invoice is posted. This means that the receivable or payable amount is reduced by the withholding tax amount.
  4. In some countries/regions, such as Argentina and Italy, some business transactions require other types of postings. Withholding tax is posted as an offsetting entry to the expense account instead of the payment amount reduced by the withholding tax amount. You make the relevant settings for this when maintaining withholding tax codes.
  5. When resetting cleared items, the clearing document is automatically reversed if it contains withholding tax data. For withholding tax types with accumulation, the accumulated base amount is adjusted if the payment document is reversed.
  6. In this blog, the two scenarios of posting withholding tax at point of payment and posting withholding tax at point of payment are shown in the examples for Account Payable.

 

  • Configure Withholding Tax in the System

1. Activate Withholding Tax - 104228

To use withholding tax in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (also known as extended withholding tax), you must first activate it.

2. Define Withholding Tax Keys – 106576

Withholding tax codes are used to identify different withholding tax types.

3. Define Withholding Tax Type for Payment Posting – 104226

Withholding taxes can be deducted at two points in time; they can be deducted at the time of invoicing or at payment. Therefore, for this withholding tax type, one withholding tax type is created for payments and one for invoices.

4. Define Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting – 104225

Withholding taxes can be deducted at two points in time; they can be deducted at the time of invoicing or at payment. Therefore, for this withholding tax type, one withholding tax type is created for payments and one for invoices.

5. Assign Withholding Tax Types to Company Code – 103914

6. Define Withholding Tax Codes - 104227

In the withholding tax codes, maintain different tax rates and maintain the basis for the corresponding deductions.

7. Define Min/Max Amounts for Withholding Codes– 103915

If required, you can define a minimum/maximum limit amount for which the withholding tax code is valid, or, if no amount is specified, there is no limit to the lower and lower amount for withholding tax.

8. Assign Withholding Tax key to Withholding Tax Code – 105553

9. Assign Exchange Rate Type for Withholding Tax Type - 104400

10. Automatic Account Determination for Withholding Tax Code– 100297

11. Check Withholding Tax Configuration

①Check Company Code

②Check Business Partner

③Check Withholding Tax Type

④Simulate Calculation

 

  • Withholding Tax Demos

Withholding tax in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is also called extended withholding tax and contains withholding tax types and withholding tax codes. The withholding tax type represents the base calculation rule. The withholding tax code represents a specific feature of these rules, especially the percentage rate.

If more than one withholding tax is required for a particular transaction, this is covered in the SAP system by defining several withholding tax types. When you enter line items, you can enter withholding tax data for each withholding tax type. Note, however, that if you have two or more sets of withholding tax codes and types in an invoice, you must decide whether all the proposed withholding tax codes and types are relevant for the transaction. If you do not calculate and post some of the proposed withholding tax, you must remove the transaction-independent withholding tax code.

1. Scenario: Post Withholding Tax at Point of Invoice

1). Maintain Business Partner Master Data

From the screenshot above, you can see that the vendor withholding tax type is set as the point in time of invoice posting to generate withholding tax line items with withholding tax code T1 that is 20% withholding tax rate.

2). Post Supplier Invoice

View the financial entries generated by the simulation:

You will see that a withholding tax document entry was generated to deduct the payable amount.

2. Scenario: Post Withholding Tax at Point of Payment

1). Maintain Business Partner Master Data

From the screenshot above, you can see that the vendor withholding tax type is set as the point of payment to generate withholding tax line items with withholding tax code T1, which is 20% withholding tax rate.

2). Post Supplier Invoice

View the financial entries generated by the simulation:

3). Payment of Supplier Invoice

Simulate the payment document and you can see that at the time of payment generates the withholding tax line items in the following figure.

Currently, for the withholding tax posting point in Taiwan, customers can choose the corresponding scenario as per their requirements.

 

  • Report for Withholding Tax

For some countries/regions, the withholding tax report is generated based on the information collected from supplier and customer documents, such as cleared invoices, supplier credit memos, down payments, and cleared credit memos. The system generates a text file that can be submitted to the government authorities. Once the Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) feature is active for some countries/regions, you can use the Run Statutory Reports to generate withholding tax report.

For example, the withholding tax report for the United States is as follows:

Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report for the US

The withholding tax reporting information for Belgium is as follows:

Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

However, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not yet provide a standard withholding tax declaration report for Taiwan and user can use the following CDS View to get the corresponding withholding tax information:

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax: Supplier Withholding Tax
  • I_CustomerWithTax: Customer Withholding Tax
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode: withholding tax code
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01 : Withholding Tax on Supplier Invoice

Customers already sent requirement of withholding tax declaration reports to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition product departments and look forward to the withholding tax declaration report delivered by SAP in the future localization functionality for Taiwan. Please refer to the following link to check detail request on customer influence portal:

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

Starting with CE2502, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a new application, Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis, which answers the question, “How do you calculate withholding tax for this transaction?”, as follows is an example of a German company code:

For information about the app, refer to the help document: Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis | SAP Help Portal

However, the app is currently not supported for Taiwan and prompts the following information:

Hope the app will be open for Taiwan in the future.

 

  • Conclusion

In the Taiwan region, withholding tax is applicable for different income types, such as dividends, rents, interest, and royalties generated internally in Taiwan. It is important to ensure timely taxation, simplify tax management, and enforce compliance.

The withholding agent shall, prior to the tenth day of each month, pay to the Treasury the taxes withheld the previous month and make out withholding certificates and submit them to the tax offices not later than the January of the following tax year, specifying the payment made and the tax withheld in the previous year. In the case that three national holidays occur in immediate succession in January the period for the submission of the withholding certificates shall be extended to February 5 .For income paid to non-residents or foreign businesses having no permanent establishment in the ROC, the withholding agent shall pay to the Treasury the taxes withheld and file a withholding report and the receipts with the tax offices within ten days after withholding.

Reference link [4]

Like many tax types in Taiwan, misunderstandings of tax declarations or missed deadlines can cause economic losses for foreign companies inside and outside Taiwan.

 

  • Reference Information
  1. Help Portal Documentation | Withholding Tax
  2. Virtual Data Model and CDS Views | Withholding Tax for Supplier Invoice
  3. Taiwan withholding Tax Guide | Tax | SongjerCPA
  4. When shall a holding agent file returns and make payments of tax withheld?
  5. Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report in the US
  6. Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

entry.title: Planning in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Import Planned Revenue and Cost
entry.content:
Background

Most SAP implementation partners and some SAP customers have experiences with SAP ECC system, which has some planning features such as sales and operation planning; costing-based COPA planning; cost center expense planning; cost center activity quantity planning; and planned activity rate calculation etc. While these legacy features still exist in S/4HANA private edition (deactivated by default), they are no longer available in S/4HAHA Public Cloud. Similar requirements can be easily achieved in SAP Analytics Cloud Planning and in S&OP, Business Integration Planning, then planned data can be exported to S/4HANA through SAP standard integration, alternatively; planning activities can be done in a non-SAP system, and post plan data to S/4HANA Cloud either via excels or API. In this series of blogs, we are going to introduce the planning and budgeting features in S/4HANA Cloud, interfaces, and integration with SAC Planning.

These are three ways to populate plan data in S/4HANA Cloud.

  1. Fiori app Import Financial Plan Data
  2. Financial Plan Data-Write API
  3. Integration with SAC Planning

In this blog, we are going to introduce 1st option of import plan data via excels.

System Walkthrough

Open Fiori app Import Financial Plan Data and click download templates. There is a list of templates. We are going to use Market Segment Planning to upload planned revenue and cost.

Please note more fields can be added to the default template. Refer to Help Portal Fields for Financial Plan Data Import. I have inserted Profit center and Sold Material as additional characteristics for planned revenue and cost. Assuming we have finished planning activities outside SAP system and have converted the planned data into the required excel format.

Choose Browse CSV Files and select the target excel file. You can first test import before hitting the Import Source File button.

Jump to Display Financial Plan Data to view the results.

You can also use Fiori app Market Segments - Plan/Actual to view the uploaded planned revenue and cost of goods sold.

Summary

This blog has mentioned the different ways to populate financial plan data in S/4HANA Cloud and demonstrated how to upload plan data using excel templates. In the next blog, we are going to show how to send plan data to S/4HANA Cloud using API.

Feel free to leave questions or comments and explore other content and blogs. Your participation to the community is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Planning in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Post Cost Center Planning Expenses via API
entry.content:
Introducing Plan data API

As mentioned in previous blog. These are three ways to populate plan data in S/4HANA Cloud.

  1. Fiori app Import Financial Plan Data
  2. Financial Plan Data-Write API
  3. Integration with SAC Planning

We illustrated option 1 of using excel files to upload revenue and cost data via Fiori app Import Financial Plan Data. In this blog, we are going to explore how option 2 works by posting cost center planned expenses via API as an example.  Please refer to API definition  Financial Planning Data - Write | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

System Walkthrough

Firstly, let’s go through a series of steps to set up communication arrangement for the API.

Fiori App Maintain Communication Users to create an API user.

Fiori App Communication Systems to create a communication system and maintain the user created in previously step.

Fiori App Communication Arrangements to create a communication arrangement with communication scenario SAP_COM_0087(SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning Integration) and maintain the communication system created in previous step.

A list of inbound services is associated with this communication arrangement, the one of our interests for this blog is Financial Planning Data – Write.

In the payload, we are going to post some office building rent expense to an overhead cost center for period 1 and 2 in year 2025.

After successfully posting the planning data via API, we are navigating to Fiori app Cost Center Plan/Actual to view the results. As can be seen, planned amount in company code currency has been populated for cost center under the G/L account office/Building Rent for period 1 and 2 in year 2025.

Summary

Please note the Financial Planning Data – Write API can be used to post a variety of planned data; we have only used cost center expenses for demonstration purpose in this blog. The API can be implemented together with SAP CPI to achieve greater flexibility and obtain more benefits. The idea is that with this API, it is possible to conduct planning activities in a non-SAP software and post prepared plan data to S/4HANA system for further processing and comparing plan and actual.

In the next blog, we will continue our planning and budgeting topics and allocate the planned expenses for overhead cost center to other cost centers based on certain criteria.

Feel free to leave questions or comments and explore other content and blogs. Your participation to the community is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Planning in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Statistical Plan Data & Cost Center Allocation
entry.content:
In the previous blog.  We illustrated how to use Financial Plan Data Write API to post overhead expense to a cost center.  In this blog we are continuing our planning and budgeting topics, and we are going to allocate the overhead expense to several cost centers based on planned statistical key figure values.

System Walkthrough

In our demo, there are five cost centers; overhead cost center will be the sender to allocate the expense to the rest of cost centers based on planned statistical key figures.

Open Fiori App Import Statistical Key Figure Plan Data and choose Cost Center Statistical Key Figures template.

Fill in the template with cost centers and their respective statistical values. In our example, 1001 is number of employees.

In Fiori App Import Statistical Key Figure Plan Data, click Browse CSV files to select the excel template prepared, hit Import Source File to import plan data.

To check the results, as for CE2502, there is no Fiori app to do so directly. A custom consumption CDS view based I_FinStatisticalKeyFigureItem can be created using Fiori app Custom Analytical Queries.

Open Fiori App Manage allocations to create cost center allocations. Maintain Receiver Rule: Variable portions; Sender Rule: Posted amounts; and maintain other attributes accordingly.

In Receiver Basis tab, choose SKF-1 as statistical plan data, and maintain other attributes accordingly.

Run the Allocation and view the results.

 

Summary

We have demonstrated the process steps to import planned statistical key figures and based on their values allocating cost center expenses. So far with previous blogs, we have posted planned revenue, cost and expenses, in the next blog, we are going to custom develop a report via embedded Analytics Fiori apps to view the planned data and comparing with actuals.

Feel free to leave questions or comments and explore other content and blogs. Your participation to the community is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Exploring Deployment Models for Acquisitions Using SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Customer Success Day - NYC - March 2025.Recently, I had the opportunity to attend an event in New York City where I presented to a group of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers on the current status of SAP’s two-tier strategy. During the event, several companies demonstrated interest and raised questions regarding strategies for new business acquisitions utilizing the two-tier approach. In this blog, I intend to share a few simple ideas that can help provide deployment models to our customers.

 

General Status of the Two-Tier Strategy

The two-tier strategy is well documented in the SAP Signavio Process Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, providing considerable information on this topic. SAP offers over 50 two-tier scenarios, encompassing approximately 68 distinct scope item elements in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Traditionally, we created these two-tier scenarios to drive hybrid deployments of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private or on-premise editions for corporate companies, while using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for subsidiaries. Now, this concept supports all possible deployment combinations, including corporate companies utilizing the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

SAP Signavio Process Navigator - Two-Tier ERP.

Customers should refer to the deck for detailed information on the two-tier strategy in the SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

SAP Signavio Process Navigator - Two-Tier ERP Filters.

Customers can apply the "Two-Tier ERP" filter in the SAP Process Navigator to view all relevant scope items involved in two-tier end-to-end scenarios (this is available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition). For additional information on Two-Tier ERP, you may refer to the SAP Two-Tier ERP community at https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/s4hana-cloud/two-tier-erp

I would now like to introduce two models for companies engaged in acquisitions utilizing SAP S/4HANA. While there are additional models to consider, these two provide a foundational understanding of the deployment strategies and potential options available.

 

Acquisitions When Corporate Company Is in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

In cases where the corporate company operates on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (or SAP S/4HANA on-premise), there are several options for integrating new acquisitions. Companies can either integrate the new business into the current corporate system or deploy the new company in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

We increasingly see customers favoring the use of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition due to its speed, independence, low total cost of ownership (TCO), agile innovation cycles (including upgrades), system operations managed by SAP, and rapid implementation capabilities. This aligns with a fit-to-standard approach using SAP Best Practices to bring newly acquired businesses on board. These new businesses are typically deployed in a two-tier architecture with the corporate system, ensuring agility and swift integration. Two-tier scenarios are often recommended to connect these systems, particularly with a focus on financial integration, depending on their level of independence from the corporate structure.

Two-Tier ERP for Corporate Company in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition acquiring new companies using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Let me provide an example of a two-tier process: for financial consolidation while maintaining independence, companies can use financial consolidation (1SG) with group reporting capabilities to manage financial reporting while allowing the business to operate independently. This approach provides business agility in response to constant market changes that the new business might experience.

Example - Group Reporting - Financial Consolidation.

If the corporate entity continues to acquire new businesses that they plan to retain long-term, they can use the same public cloud instance to add new companies as company codes, thereby benefiting from the same public cloud instance and contract. As of version 2502, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition covers 59 standard countries in 33 languages (by version 2508, we plan—subject to change—to cover 60 standard countries with 34 languages) and 159 non-standard countries using the Configuration Localization Toolkit as part of localization as self-service (in a 3-system landscape only). Therefore, adding international acquisitions should not pose a problem given the system's ample capabilities to meet compliance requirements.

Corporate Company can use same public cloud instance to add new companies as company codes.

If this corporate entity is looking to acquire many new businesses internationally and provide them with independence, we could also consider a federated architecture with different SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants distributed by geography to address concerns like data residency, latency, and other related issues. However, I won't cover this in this blog.

For turnaround acquisitions—if the plan is to enhance operations and later spin off the business in the short term—it is advisable to obtain a new cloud subscription and operate this business in a separate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition landscape. Please discuss with your account executive directly to explore your options for adding a new subscription. If the new subscription is added under the same customer number (sold-to-party), please request that the new SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environment has a different "provision-to-party." This strategy allows for the rapid and independent onboarding of a new acquisition, aligning with SAP Best Practices. The speed of the spin-off is facilitated as it becomes a contract ownership transfer, enabling the transition of the subscription to the new business while maintaining operational autonomy and providing a platform for future growth without requiring a burdensome technical setup for the carve-out of the business.

Corporate Company in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - For turnaround acquisitions - it is advisable to obtain a new cloud subscription and operate this business in a separate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition landscape.

Acquisitions When Corporate Company Is in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

For corporate entities already utilizing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, integrating acquisitions can be streamlined within the same cloud environment. This approach facilitates a uniform deployment model across the corporate and acquired entities, enabling consistent processes and a unified system landscape.

I have observed an increasing trend among our customers using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, where they expand their current system by adding new countries and company codes. This model offers several advantages, including reduced integration costs, streamlined governance, rapid implementation using SAP Best Practices, and expedited onboarding processes. Companies can leverage the existing public cloud infrastructure to extend their operations to newly acquired entities, ensuring business continuity and operational efficiency.

SAP is evaluating options (plans subject to change) to facilitate a templated approach for organizational structure and configuration using SAP Central Business Configuration to accelerate the creation of company codes.

Corporate Company in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - integrating acquisitions can be streamlined within the same cloud environment.

For turnaround acquisitions, if the intention is to enhance operations and later spin off the business, it is recommended to obtain a new cloud contract and operate this business in a separate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition landscape. Please discuss with your account executive directly to explore your options for adding a new subscription. If the new subscription is added under the same customer number (sold-to-party), please request that the new SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environment has a different "provision-to-party." This strategy allows for rapid and independent onboarding of a new acquisition, aligning with SAP Best Practices. The speed of the spin-off is facilitated as it becomes a contract ownership transfer, enabling the transition of the subscription to the new business while maintaining operational autonomy and providing a platform for future growth without requiring a burdensome technical setup for the carve-out of the business.

Corporate Company in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - For turnaround acquisitions, it is recommended to obtain a new cloud contract and operate this business in a separate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition landscape.

SAP is evaluating future scenarios for company code carve-outs that might offer additional capabilities for the rapid spin-off of company codes operating in the same system instance (this can be done today with a specific IT consulting project). However, at the current stage with release 2502, the most agile approach is to build a company code in a separate instance if you want a turnkey solution for a quick spin-off of the turnaround acquisition company.

In conclusion, SAP’s two-tier strategy provides flexible and efficient deployment models for companies navigating acquisitions. Whether the corporate entity utilizes SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the two-tier approach ensures rapid integration and sustained agility. I encourage our customers to explore the detailed scenarios and models available in the SAP Signavio Process Navigator to fully leverage the benefits of the two-tier strategy.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by commenting on this blog.

More Information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, release info here
    • Product videos on our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA YouTube playlist
    • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here
    • Early Release Webinar Series here
    • Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Podcast here
    • openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA here
    • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, here
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Community: here
    • Feature Scope Description here
    • What’s New here
    • Help Portal Product Page here

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia

entry.title: GenAI Expert Enablement Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud: please meet EDITH
entry.content:
Dear Cloud ERP Community! 

Today, we’re excited to share a behind-the-scenes secret about the enablement team for Cloud ERP—the team I have the privilege of leading. Our mission? To craft a comprehensive set of enablement materials designed to educate and empower our Cloud ecosystem. From in-depth product and release blogs to engaging webinars on SAP Learning, insightful project podcasts, and expert discussions, we provide a wealth of content to support our community. Most of our content can be found here.

As with any content creation process, GenAI has the potential to be a game-changer—not just in boosting efficiency, but also in enhancing the quality of our materials. Plus, it allows content creators to focus more on creativity and innovation rather than tedious manual tasks.

That’s where our big idea came in: harnessing SAP technology to develop our very own expert content creation tool. We call it EDITH. This groundbreaking GenAI-powered project, led by Sang Dao and Jonas Wang, went from concept to go-live in an astonishingly short span of just six months.

In this blog, I’ll be sitting down with our product experts, Sang and Jonas, to dive into the business cases behind EDITH, the technology that powers it, and some firsthand feedback from our end-users. Let’s get started!

Yannick: First a question to Sang, can you please share the key capabilities of EDITH? 

Sang: Sure! Edith is a groundbreaking GenAI-powered enablement tool designed to revolutionize how SAP experts approach customer go-live projects, product enablement, and adoption strategies. This is an example of how SAP uses SAP technology to improve internal content creation processes. Edith not only simplifies the complexities of knowledge management but also empowers experts to deliver exceptional customer experiences at scale. 

Edith stands out as a versatile tool, integrating advanced GenAI technology to address the needs of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product experts. Here’s a closer look at its core capabilities: 

1. Enablement Asset Creation at Scale 

  • Generate diverse enablement content, such as release highlight blogs, product deep-dive articles, podcasts, webinar scripts, and session summaries. 
  • Streamline the creation process with minimal manual intervention by leveraging Edith’s integration with the SAP Help Portal and other knowledge bases.

2. Most Asked Question (MAQ) Analysis 

  • Quickly identify trending topics and frequently asked questions within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud community. 
  • Use insights to address common customer pain points and enhance support strategies. 

3. Consultative Question Answering 

  • Provide precise and actionable answers to complex customer and partner queries during project implementation. 
  • Support critical go-live initiatives with contextual responses sourced from SAP’s robust knowledge base. 

To understand more about the personas, the value propositions and see demos, you can check this Interactive Value Journey: Link  

Yannick: What challenges in product enablement does Edith help solve? 

Sang: The journey to seamless product enablement and customer support is often fraught with challenges. Here’s how Edith effectively tackles them: 

Challenges 

Edith’s Solutions 

Complex customer and partner questions during project implementation. 

Draft accurate answers from the SAP knowledge base with unparalleled precision. 

Rising number of “how-to” queries in the SAP Community. 

Analyze trending topics with just two clicks, offering timely and relevant insights. 

Manual effort in producing enablement assets. 

Automate content creation using GenAI and a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach. 

 Yannick: Now, let’s shift to the technical side. Jonas, can you walk us through the architecture and key design principles behind Edith? 

Jonas: Of course! EDITH is a cloud-native application developed using BTP technologies and services. At its core, EDITH is constructed with the Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), which is a framework comprising languages, libraries, and tools for crafting enterprise-grade cloud applications following established best practices. 

A major technical highlight of EDITH is its ability to enhance responses through Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), a key approach to improving the reliability and accuracy of generative AI outputs. 

Yannick: Why does Edith need RAG, and how does it work? 

Jonas: Most Large Language Models (LLMs) are fine-tuned for chat functionality, where they respond to questions by generating the most suitable answer with limited knowledge. This often results in a common issue known as hallucinations, particularly when questions pertain to topics beyond the LLMs' training datasets. 

To tackle this challenge, EDITH employs Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The concept of RAG involves: 

  1. Retrieving relevant context based on semantic similarity to the user query. 
  2. Combining this context with the prompt before directing it to the LLMs, ensuring that responses remain grounded in factual data. 

This significantly reduces errors and ensures that responses align with SAP’s extensive knowledge base. 

Yannick: How does Edith manage and retrieve relevant knowledge efficiently? 

Jonas: EDITH utilizes the vector store capability of HANA Cloud, which is designed to store relevant factual data (ground truths) and retrieve data appropriate for queries. An integration flow regularly retrieves data from various sources and converts them into vector embeddings before storing them in the HANA Cloud database. 

When a user submits a query, Edith searches this vector database for semantically similar content, ensuring precise and context-aware responses. 

Yannick: How does Edith integrate with SAP’s AI infrastructure? 

Jonas: SAP's approach for Generative AI applications recommends interacting with LLMs via the SAP Generative AI Hub's LLM Access Service on SAP BTP. This service acts as a central layer for control, trust, and orchestration, as well as providing tools for exploration and evaluation. 

By following SAP’s best practices, Edith: 

  • Interfaces with LLMs through deployments created within the LLM Access Service. 
  • Ensures secure, scalable AI interactions across different teams. 
  • Leverages SAP-approved AI models for enterprise-grade performance. 

Yannick: Finally, what’s next for Edith? 

Sang: We are continuously refining Edith’s capabilities to enhance enablement strategies across SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Our goal is to: 

  • Expand integration with additional SAP services. 
  • Improve user experience based on feedback from product experts. 
  • Introduce more automation in content creation to further streamline enablement processes. 

With Edith, SAP is pioneering a new era of AI-driven enablement, ensuring that product experts have the tools they need to deliver exceptional customer experiences at scale. 

Yannick: Sang and Jonas, thanks for sharing your expertise. it is great to see your entrepreneurial spirit, and the clear outcome of EDITH not only for the SAP colleagues, but as well for the Cloud ERP stakeholders and are the community members interested in our product assets.

To our readers: this tool is internal and not for sale. With this article we aim to inspire SAP experts curious in GenAI solutions and motive them to turn their idea into execution, leveraging the SAP technology. I am very interested in your ideas. Eventually it is a good topic for a podcast Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Reach out!

🔎Find All of Our Assets  

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

Click to open the SAP BTP Enablement Wheel

#GenAI #SAP #RAG

entry.title: 资产会计 - SAP S4HANA Cloud（公共版）2502 版本中的资产重估新功能介绍
entry.content:
背景介绍

我们会计记录的都是历史成本，但是随着社会经济的发展，会计上记录的过去的金额不能反映真实公允的价值了，因此准则允许企业进行重估。例如2010年工厂购买的商业地产，记录在账面上的价值是30万元，但是2020的时候价值就变成了60万，那么现在账面上记录的30万元就不能真实公允的体现出实际的价值，所以需要重估。

对于手动资产重估，可使用“过账资产重估”应用程序 (F7775)。之前，您可以同时以所有或多个货币重估资产。通常，以多种货币执行重估。

但是，在某些情况下，您只需要以本币执行重估，而不会影响集团货币（硬通货币）。

新业务交易类型“以所选货币重估”可用于此目的。使用此新业务交易类型，可以单一（所选）货币过账重估并报告。

这意味着您现在可以区分标准重估情况（所有受影响的货币）和特定重估（所有分类账中只有一种货币）。

测试场景

资产于 2024 年 12 月 31 日购置，折旧码为 CD01。使用年限为 4 年。折旧方法为直线折旧。残值率百分比为 5%

购置和生产成本交易(APC):

100,000

2025年计划普通折旧:

-23,750

2025年底净账面值:

76,250

资产购置之后

2025年的计划折旧金额的计算公式是100000*(1-5%)/48*12=23750

由于通货膨胀，需要在 2024 年末进行重估：资产的账面价值将重估 20%。

在过账资产重估应用的选择屏幕上，指定以下值：

例如：

  • 20,000 APC 的重估
  • 5000 的普通折旧的重估

对于手动重估，可以使用过账资产重估应用 (F7775)。之前，您可以使用该应用同时以所有或多个货币重估。通常以多种货币执行重估。

但是，在某些情况下，您需要仅以本币执行重估，而集团货币（硬通货币）不受影响。

2502版本支持对于特定重估（所有分类账中仅一种货币），在字段业务交易类型选择“重估(所选货币)”

资产购置和重估之后

经过重估之后，2025年的计划折旧金额的计算公式是[(100000+20000）*(1-5%)-5000)/48*12=27250

除手动重估外，自 2502 版本起，新应用维护资产重估指数引入了有关在特定折旧范围中使用重估指数自动重估的功能。

如果要定期执行基于指数值的自动重估，则使用此应用。

可在链接中找到详细说明

  1. 创建重估索引（强制）

2.将索引标识分配到资产主记录（必填）

3.检查固定资产评估视图中的价值（可选）

4.检查资产历史数据表中的值（可选）

此处共享步骤的一些结果:

步骤 1：创建重估索引（必填）

步骤 2：将索引标识分配到资产主记录（必填）

步骤 3：检查管理固定资产应用的评估视图中的值（可选）

根据分配给资产的重估索引，重估金额和折旧金额将按如下设计进行计划。

对于2024年

对于2025年

对于2026年

对于2027年

步骤 4：检查资产历史数据表中的值（可选）

总结

这一新功能使得管理资产重估更加灵活，使您能够更有效地处理特定重估场景。

entry.title: Planning in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - embedded analytics of plan and actual report
entry.content:
In previous blogs, we have posted planned revenue, cost and expenses, in this blog, we are going to custom develop a report via embedded Analytics Fiori apps to view the planned data and comparing with actuals.

System Walkthrough

Mockup report is going to be a condensed income statement plan actual comparison report.

Two CDS Views can be used, I_ActualPlanJrnlEntryItemCube and I_ActualPlanLineItemSemTag. Latter has some advantages as it contains semantic tags, together with financial statement versions offers easy maintenance when it comes to assign G/L accounts to reporting items. But for the demo purpose we will use the first one. Open Fiori app Custom Analytical Queries or the new app Custom Analytical Queries and Services. Choose Data Source.

In Field Selection, select fields of our interests

In Display, create Characteristics Structure, for example maintain Plan Category ACT01 for ActualAmount and Plan Category PLN for PlanAmount, alternatively, use Plan Category as an input filter.

Create Restrictive measures. For example: “Net Sales:” is just placeholder reporting item, we can use any fixed value with “*”.

For measure “Products”, we maintain G/L Account values based on Amount in Company Code Currency

Create calculated measures

After selecting the needed fields, creating all the necessary measures, setting up filters. Hit preview to view the report as a multidimensional report. At this point, the CDS view can be published and saved into a business catalogue, and the report can be operational.

To take advantage of embedded SAC features, use Fiori App Manage KPIs and Reports to further process the report. Select the CDS View created.

Create a table with the CDS view and adjust the layout, this is the same as in multidimensional report, some visual representations can be added here (not shown in the demo).

I created another page with G/L account (with FSV selection) as rows and standard measures in columns. This is what a standard P&L report would look like.

Summary

We have showed how to custom a report to view plan data. For more information about embedded Analytics features and more use cases, please refer to my colleagues’ blogs. Customer use case of Embedded SAC on Account Balan... - SAP Community

Feel free to leave questions or comments and explore other content and blogs. Your participation to the community is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Creating a CBO with CRUD operations | SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction
Creating a Custom Business Object (CBO) in Public Cloud, from the initial setup to enabling CRUD operations. This includes communication scenario, arrangement, and catalog association. A simple process to enable customizations in the environment.


Step 1 - Creating the CBO (Custom Business Object)
Access the Custom Business Object app.



Create a new CBO.

 

 

 


To enable future CRUD access, select this option.
Add the required fields, define data types and keys, then click Publish.


Step 2 - Creating the Communication Scenario
Access the Custom Communication Scenarios app.


Create a new Communication Scenario.

 

 




Define a name and description.

Save and publish.





Step 3 - Creating the Communication Arrangement
Access the Communication Arrangements app.



Create a new Communication Arrangement.

 

 




Associate it with the previously created Communication Scenario.



Select the Communication System (e.g., ZLOCAL, already pointing to the environment).

 

 




Publish.


Step 4 - Linking Communications to the CBO
Open the previously created CBO.
In the Inbound Services tab, add the created service.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 



The Communication Arrangement is now associated.

 



Step 5 - Assigning the CBO to a Business Catalog
Access the Custom Catalog Extensions app. 



Locate the two objects that need to be linked to a Business Catalog (IBS and SCBO).

 






 

 



 

 




Publish.


Step 6 - Accessing the CBO CRUD (SM30 Equivalent)
1- Accessing through the CBO. Click in Go To Generated UI

 



2- Searching via apps.

 



Step 7 - Result
Click in Create to create a record.

entry.title: Releasing "requests" | In-App Mode (Custom Objects) | SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In Public Cloud, the customization transport process differs from Private Cloud (STMS). The request is now called Collection. When transporting objects in In-App mode, each modification generates a new version within the Software Collection. You can add only the modified items, include comments, and use filters to streamline the search process. After importing the collection into the Test environment, performing the Forward action is necessary to make it available for import into PRD.

Step 1
Access the Export Software Collection app.



Create a Software Collection (if one does not already exist for the relevant topic or module/organization in the environment).


Name your Software Collection and click Create.

Once the Software Collection is created, note that it is versioned. This means that with every object modification and transport, a new version is automatically created for transport.




In Add Items, you can select the objects to be included in this Collection for transport.

 

 

 


Support Filters are available to assist with object searches within the environment.



After adding the desired objects, click Check to verify if there are any blockers, dependencies, or inconsistencies for transport.


Check Option
Note: It is possible to transport only the modified items or all items again. The first option is always recommended.



A log is generated at the end, displaying the transport status.


 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Export Option – This is where the release (Export) to the Test environment actually takes place.

 

A comment can be added for each versioned transport.



A log is generated at the end, displaying the transport status.

 

 

 

 

 




Step 2
After exporting, log in to the Test environment and access the Import Collection app.


Use the available filters to search for the desired collection. Upon reaching the Test environment, the versioned collection will have the status "Ready for Import."

 

 

 

 

 


Select the desired versioned collection and choose Check and Import.

 

 

 

 

 



Add a transport comment.



Click Manual Import.

The import process starts, and the collection status changes to "Importing" during processing.


Once the import is complete, a confirmation message is displayed:


Step 3
The collection has only been imported into the Test environment but is not yet available for import into the Production environment. To make it available for import into PRD, it is necessary to perform the Forward action.

 

 

 

 



Select the collection and click Forward.




The collection is forwarded to the Production environment transport path.

 

 

 

 

 


To finalize, log in to the Production environment and access the Import Collection app.




Select the desired collection and import it.



The collection has been successfully imported into the PRD environment.

entry.title: Transportando "requests" | Modo In-app | SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introdução

Em Public Cloud, o processo de transporte de customizações é diferente de como fazemos em Private Cloud (STMS). A antiga request agora é chamada de Collection. Ao transportar objetos no modo In-App, cada modificação gera uma nova versão dentro de uma Software Collection. É possível adicionar apenas os itens modificados, incluir comentários e usar filtros para facilitar a busca. Após importar a Collection para o ambiente de Test, é necessário executar a ação Forward para disponibilizá-la para importação no PRD.


Passo 1
Acessar app Export Software Collection



Criar uma Software Collection (caso ainda não tenha referente ao tema ou como foi modularizado/organizado no ambiente)


Nomear sua Software Collection e clicar em Create

Software Collection criada. Repare que ela é versionada, ou seja, a cada mudança de objeto e transporte, a mesma cria uma nova versão para transporte


Em Add Items, podemos adicionar os objetos que queremos incluir nessa Collection para transporte



Filtros disponibilizados de apoio para pesquisa no ambiente



Após inserir os objetos desejados, podemos clicar em Check para verificar se há algum impeditivo, dependência ou inconsistência para transporte

 

Opção Check
OBS: Temos a opção de transportar somente o que foi alterado, ou todos os itens novamente. Recomenda-se sempre a primeira opção



Ao final é gerado uma log com o status do transporte



Opção Export – É onde de fato acontece a liberação (Export) para o ambiente de Test

OBS: Temos a opção de transportar somente o que foi alterado, ou todos os itens novamente. Recomenda-se sempre a primeira opção.


Podemos inserir um comentário referente ao transporte a cada versionamento


Ao final é gerado uma log com o status do transporte

 


Passo 2
Após exportado, logar no ambiente de Test, e acessar app Import Collection


Podemos pesquisar pelos seguintes filtros. Ao chegar no ambiente de Test, a collection do versionamento em questão estará com o status “Ready for Import”

 

Selecionar a collection do versionamento desejado e selecionar a opção Check and Import



Adicionar comentário de transporte


Clicar em Manual Import



Importação iniciada:



O status da collection mudará para Importing durante o processamento:

Ao finalizar, a mensagem

 

Passo 3
Com isso, a collection foi apenas importada para o ambiente de Test, mas não necessariamente disponibilizada para a importação ao ambiente Produtivo. Para disponibilizarmos a ser importada a PRD, precisamos fazer o Forward dela:



Selecionar collection e clicar em Forward


Collection encaminhada à trilha do ambiente produtivo



Para finalizar, logar em ambiente produtivo e acessar app Import Collection


  

Selecionar a collection que deseja importar



Collection importada em PRD

entry.title: 集锦帖：台湾地区常见本地化需求在SAP ERP公有云中的实施方式建议
entry.content:
SAP ERP公有云解决方案针对台湾地区提供了许多本地化功能，例如符合当地法规的财务报表、税务申报、电子发票管理、数据合规处理等，完整信息可参考 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition中针对台湾的本地化功能。

过去几年中，许多用户和实施商在应用该系统后给予SAP大量有益反馈。本文将聚焦其中几个重要话题，通过架构师的见解，为台湾地区的SAP应用生态圈的客户和实施伙伴提供参考，帮助企业更好地利用SAP ERP云系统推动数字化管理。

 

基于利润中心的成本核算

利润中心评估是企业财务管理中不可或缺的一部分，专注于评估各业务单位或部门的财务表现。通过将收入和成本归集到特定的利润中心，企业能够分析盈利能力、促进问责制及支持战略决策制定。目前，SAP ERP公有云暂不支持此类业务需求，参考：3371144 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud 系统中不支持利润中心评估、Profit Center Valuation | SAP Customer Influence Portal。建议企业在前期沟通中了解SAP产品现状及设计理念，并根据咨询商的业务优化建议调整企业管理方式，运用SAP提供的最佳实践。

 

外劳采购的税代扣代缴与申报

在中国台湾，通过外劳的采购来支持企业内外需求相当常见。当地法规要求企业为这类采购进行税代扣代缴，具体可参考：Comprehensive Overview of Withholding Tax in Taiwan。目前，SAP ERP公有云尚未提供全面的本地化解决方案，但可了解架构师提供的变通应用方案：SAP ERP公有云台湾本地化-代扣税实施建议。

若贵司或您的企业客户有类似需求，欢迎通过企业S-User账户登录以下页面，投下您宝贵的一票，以助SAP产品部门更好地规划相关功能的实现：Withholding Tax Request | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

台湾统一发票管理及VAT申报

根据台湾地区相关法规，目前（2025年）有效的统一发票形式共有五种。SAP ERP公有云提供了电子发票的标准端到端解决方案。关于统一发票在SAP ERP公有云中的管理建议、方案应用注意事项、及与加值中心集成申报VAT的实施方式建议，请参阅：台湾地区统一发票在 SAP ERP 公有云系统中的实施方案建议。

 

LCM报表

LCM报表是依据IFRS准则下LCNRV（成本与可变现净值孰低法，参考 IAS 2 Inventories ）库存估值方法的财务报表。根据台湾法规，已上市或准备IPO的企业需要定期披露LCM报表。目前，SAP ERP公有云中的库存估值标准APP尚未支持LCNRV方法，企业需定制报表以满足相关法规需求。如需此功能，请在以下页面投票：Inventory Valuation Enhancement for IFRS Compliance with LCNRV Method | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

库龄表

台湾上市企业需通过ERP系统的库龄表有效管理库存资产，确保合规及财务报表的准确性，改善资产流动性，减少损失，提高竞争力。SAP ERP公有云目前提供标准应用 "库存KPI分析" 供企业分析库存指标。企业或SAP实施伙伴亦可通过定制化报表满足深度定制库龄表的需求。更多相关博客可在 SAP Community 查询。

entry.title: Exciting Opportunity for SAP Partners: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative - Cycle 1, 2025
entry.content:
Dear SAP Partners,

We're thrilled to announce that the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025 is still open for your registrations! This is a unique opportunity for you to collaborate with SAP and help shape the future of our products. This is your chance to make a real impact and contribute valuable insights to the development of SAP's upcoming products, features, and applications. Don't miss out – register by March 28, 2025, to join the current projects open to SAP partners. We look forward to hearing your perspectives and ideas on the following initiatives:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Create AI-driven extensions and automations with SAP Build
  • SAP BPM, SAP BRM and SAP Process Orchestration migration to Cloud
  • SAP BTP Cockpit, Cloud Foundry UIs
  • SAP BTP Usability Event - User research project
  • SAP Integration Suite

SAP Customer Experience

  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Cloud Elastic Compute Node
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape

SAP Globalization

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report

Lifecycle Management

  • DevOps with ABAP

Others

  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX
  • Shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis

We believe collaboration is at the heart of innovation. By working together with our customers and partners, we can reshape the future of SAP products and transform the way businesses operate.

Don't wait – register today on influence.sap.com and join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud V2 & S/4HANA: Streamlining Opportunity Management & Order Fulfillment
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP offers a robust suite of tools to streamline sales and service operations. From upselling and lead management to automated order processing and post-delivery service management, SAP provides an end-to-end solution that enhances efficiency and customer satisfaction. This blog explores key features and integrations that drive seamless business processes.

A Short Story: Transforming Sales with SAP

John, a sales manager at TechSolutions Inc., faced challenges in managing leads, generating accurate quotations, and ensuring timely order fulfillment. His team often struggled with manual data entry, delayed pricing approvals, and service inefficiencies.

After implementing SAP’s Sales and Service Cloud integrated with S/4HANA, John’s team saw a transformation. AI-driven Opportunity Scores helped them focus on high-potential deals, while Product Recommendations streamlined upselling. Automated Quotation Management with Price Simulation and ATP Check reduced errors and improved customer confidence. Once an order was placed, S/4HANA Solution Orders ensured seamless coordination between products and services. Automated Delivery and Billing accelerated order processing, and post-sales support was effortlessly handled through S/4HANA Service Orders.

With SAP, John’s team increased sales efficiency, reduced turnaround times, and enhanced customer satisfaction. Now, they can focus on growing the business instead of handling repetitive tasks.

Upselling and Lead/Opportunity Creation

1. Opportunity Score, Playbook & Opportunity Summary

SAP Sales and Service Cloud leverages AI-driven Opportunity Scores to help sales teams prioritize deals. The Playbook feature offers guided selling strategies, ensuring best practices are followed. The Opportunity Summary consolidates crucial insights, enabling data-driven decision-making.

 

Quotation and Order Creation

1. Quotation + Price Simulation + ATP Check

SAP facilitates accurate Quotation Management by incorporating Price Simulation and Available-to-Promise (ATP) Check. These features help validate pricing strategies and ensure product availability before finalizing a sale.

 

 

2. S/4HANA Solution Order

S/4HANA Solution Order enables businesses to combine products and services into a single transaction, offering a unified and flexible sales approach. This ensures seamless coordination between different sales components.

3. S/4HANA Sales Order

A Sales Order in S/4HANA ensures accurate order processing, tax calculations, and stock validation in real time. This reduces manual intervention and accelerates order fulfillment.

4. S/4HC - Automated Delivery and Billing

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud (S/4HC), businesses can automate delivery and billing, ensuring timely invoicing and seamless logistics operations. This automation minimizes errors and enhances the customer experience.

 

Key Benefits of SAP's Integrated Sales and Service Solutions

  • ✔ Real-Time Insights – AI-driven analytics and scoring improve decision-making.
  • ✔ Automation & Efficiency – Automated workflows reduce manual errors and accelerate processes.
  • ✔ Seamless Integration – End-to-end integration between sales, delivery, and service ensures continuity.
  • ✔ Enhanced Customer Experience – Proactive engagement and personalized recommendations drive satisfaction.

Conclusion

SAP’s comprehensive ecosystem enhances sales performance, order processing, and service management through automation, AI-driven insights, and seamless integrations. By leveraging these advanced capabilities, businesses can boost revenue, streamline operations, and deliver a superior customer experience.

Embrace SAP’s intelligent sales and service solutions today and stay ahead in the digital transformation journey!
